name: onnozelaer-claude-marketplace
description: Personally created Claude Code plugins by Onnozelaer.

plugins.name: cabinet-of-imd
plugins.description: The Cabinet of IMD Agents — a flavour layer for Claude Code. Eight college classmates with distinct personalities, voices, disciplines, and pairings serve as specialised web-development agents. v3.0.0 sunsets all functionality (vault writes, session anchors, gate enforcement, state hooks) and keeps only character, voice, and working disciplines. Persistence is delegated to the adjudant plugin when active.

plugins.name: iteration-shelf
plugins.description: Terminal-aesthetic review boards for in-browser design iteration. Generates a curated shelf and a monster index from a JSON manifest — self-contained HTML, zero build step, on-demand iframe loading, sidebar outliner, and a warn-gate that keeps the browser from stalling on 100+ iterations. Explicit invocation only. Integrates with Superpowers and the Cabinet plugin.

plugins.name: cli-wrapper-helper
plugins.description: Build polished CLI tools — interactive bash TUIs (menus, spinners, animations) and clean Python helper scripts (sqlite readers, data reporters). Two separate skills, one consistent visual language.

plugins.name: gemineye
plugins.description: A sandboxed second opinion from a Gemini-family model via the Antigravity CLI (agy) — review-only. Eight subcommands: review, megareview, wip, sanity, name, compare, save, harvest. The reviewer is read-trusted to the project root (--add-dir) and write-sandboxed (--sandbox); it proposes edits as code blocks, Claude applies them. v0.5.0 migrates the backend off the deprecated gemini CLI (sunset 2026-06-18 for AI Pro/Ultra and free users) to agy, via a dual-CLI adapter that prefers agy and keeps a temporary gemini fallback; a follow-up release will drop gemini entirely. agy exposes no per-call model flag, so the plugin pins no model IDs. Also clears drift: removes the dead cabinet-of-imd/Bostrol coupling, retires the non-existent --file flag, and corrects the stale claim that adjudant auto-harvests at PreCompact (false since adjudant v0.7.0). Pairs with adjudant for vault context.

plugins.name: adjudant
plugins.description: v0.7.2 hardens ramasse/dream against false positives on a plugin's own templates/ scaffolds (exempt from naming, stub, index-gap checks). Vault editor/writer and project initializer. One skill, one command (/adjudant) with eight verbs (connect, port, sync, check, tidy, ramasse, dream, draw), each backed by a Python helper. v0.7.1 shares the handoff freshness layer (scripts/_handoff_freshness.py) between the PreCompact hook and the /adjudant sync verb, so a manual sync and an auto-compaction sync produce identical handoffs. v0.7.0 makes the PreCompact handoff hook mechanical-only and fresher: removes the in-hook Gemini harvest (a 30s call inside a 5s hook that was starving the handoff sync) so the hook reliably finishes its mechanical work, and adds a freshness layer — the pause tombstone now carries the NEXT action (`— next: …`), and the mirrored _handoff.md leads with a glanceable freshness header: a traffic light (green <2h / yellow 2–8h / red >8h) over real-activity age (derived from the latest .remember/today-*.md entry, not a touchable mtime), the mandatory NEXT line, and a loud STALE flag when session activity is newer than the handoff. v0.6.1 enriches /adjudant dream with three checks revived from the original /dream — unacted decisions (active decisions whose stated consequence shows no action), documentation gaps (sessions with work but no decision, stubs, briefs missing sections), and dangling scopes (brief milestones/questions never touched in sessions) — bringing the read-only comparator catalog to 10 categories. dream is fully standalone with no cross-plugin interoperation. v0.6.0 built the third cleanup tier: /adjudant dream — content/knowledge/memory refresh. dream.py walks decisions/notes/sessions and emits the read-only catalog (staleness, supersession, contradictions, redundancy clusters, stale refs, orphan questions/threads) for Claude to judge; a 5-phase superpowers flow (analyse → judge → refresh-plan → review → execute) applies the refresh with backups for destructive content ops. v0.5.2 fixes the silently-failing model name in the PreCompact harvest (gemini-3.5-flash → gemini-2.5-flash). v0.5.0 adds PreCompact Gemini harvest: before each compaction, the hook reads the CC transcript and calls Gemini (flash, sandboxed) to extract 5 durable bullets (decisions, blockers, unresolved questions) into _handoff.md. Failure-closed — any error skips harvest, existing sync runs unchanged. v0.4.0 brings: connect.py + sync.py automation, breadcrumb auto-follow, cross-machine portability, inline-code wikilink false-positive fix, hook format alignment. Locked three-tier cleanup model: tidy (surface) / ramasse (structural) / dream (semantic). Rigid spec, locked tag schema, drift-defense via pre-commit validators.
